mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production indicate immediate supply chain bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns during post-shock recovery phases, or the predictive accuracy falls below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 4.0%, higher discount rates outweigh earnings growth, compressing equity valuations and forcing a rotation out of growth stocks, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, invalidating the valuation compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor strength signal yields a negative or zero information coefficient for 60-day forward returns across mid-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding economic activity and higher corporate profit margins, which immediately improve market sentiment and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant relationship with 5-day forward S&P 500 returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 in pooled regressions.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below its 2017 baseline level, manufacturing sector weakness signals impending earnings revisions, prompting portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month during contractionary phases.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where INDPRO drops below 100, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during contractions.
